solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean backspaceCompare(String s, String t) {
        int i = s.length() - 1, j = t.length() - 1;
        while (i >= 0 || j >= 0) {
            i = getValidIndex(s, i);
            j = getValidIndex(t, j);
            if (i >= 0 && j >= 0 && s.charAt(i) != t.charAt(i)) return false;
            if ((i >= 0 && j < 0) || (i < 0 && j >= 0)) return false;
            i--;
            j--;
        }
        return true;
    }

    private int getValidIndex(String s, int index) {
        int backspaceCount = 0;
        while (index >= 0) {
            if (s.charAt(index) == '#') {
                backspaceCount++;
            } else if (backspaceCount > 0) {
                backspaceCount--;
            } else {
                break;
            }
            index--;
        }
        return index;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution processes both strings from the end to the beginning, simulating the effect of backspaces in-place without using extra memory. It compares characters only after resolving all backspace operations, achieving O(n) time and O(1) space complexity. The main strength is its efficiency and adherence to the follow-up constraints. The weakness is that it requires careful handling of indices and backspace logic.
